content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://huggingface.co/blog/rlhf", "source_title": "Illustrating Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "There are many applications such as writing stories where you want creativity, pieces of informative text which should be truthful, or code ...", "direct_quote": "There are many applications such as writing stories where you want creativity, pieces of informative text which should be truthful, or code snippets that we want to be executable.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "To compensate for the shortcomings of the loss itself people define metrics that are designed to better capture human preferences such as BL...", "direct_quote": "To compensate for the shortcomings of the loss itself people define metrics that are designed to better capture human preferences such as BLEU or ROUGE.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Wouldn't it be great if we use human feedback for generated text as a measure of performance or go even one step further and use that feedba...", "direct_quote": "Wouldn't it be great if we use human feedback for generated text as a measure of performance or go even one step further and use that feedback as a loss to optimize the model?", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
